english: to pass actually through it (the saints not being subject to the physical laws of matter) Mil., Thgr.; to shine, to light through; tál-byúṅ-du (thal byung du) _ogró-ba ('gro ba) to go straightforward, to act without ceremony or disguise Dzl. 252, 3; tál-ma (thal ma) Sch., tál-le (thal le) C., through and through; tál-gyis (thal gyis) directly, straightway, unhesitatingly Mil. — 4. to come or get to, to arrive at (W. *tál-ċe* (*thal ce*)), tál-nas (thal nas) lo ysum (gsum) lon three years have elapsed since they arrived; ṕa-má (pha ma) gar tál-bai (thal bai) tol-méd (tol med); bzaṅ-tál (bzang thal) safe arrival Thgr.; yár-gyi (yar gyi) bzáṅ-tál (bzang thal) čě́n-por (chen por) _ogró-ba ('gro ba) to arrive at, attain to (a blessed state) in a pleasant and speedy manner Thgr. — 5. to be over, past, finished, done, tál-lo (thal lo) of a song: it is over, finished Mil.; drúg-ču (drug cu) tál-lo (thal lo) the number of sixty is full; yál-nas (yal nas) tál-ba (thal ba) Mil. having disappeared, vanished; stór-te (stor te) (or stór-nas (stor nas)) tál (thal) he is undone, it is all over with him Mil. frq.; rim-gyis (rim gyis) je nyúṅ (je nyung) je nyúṅ (je nyung) tál (thal) by degrees it vanishes, dies away Mil.; sṅar čad-tsig (chad tshig) tál (thal) the former agreement is no longer valid; tál (thal) soṅ (song) col. = tsar (tshar) soṅ (song). — Tar. 46, 5. 12? 172, 5: tál-gyur-pa (thal gyur pa) Schf. follower, adherent, or the name of a certain sect.

the palm of the hand, tál-mo (thal mo) sbyár-ba (sbyar ba) to hold together the palms of the hands, as a gesture of devotion; tál-mo (thal mo) snún-pa (snun pa) Dzl., more frq. tál-lcág (thal lcag) rgyáb-pa (rgyab pa) to give a slap on the face, a box on the ear; tál-brdáb-pa (thal brdab pa) to clap with the hands Sch.
v. tí-gu (thi gu); ti-gu-kro-bo (thi gu khro bo) (?) C. = *'ar-gón* (*'ar gon*) W.
1. wood-pigeon, stock-dove Sch.; ti-bo (thi bo) plover, peewit, lapwing Sch. — 2. C. = té-ba (the ba).

prob. from ti-gu (thi gu), 1. carpenter's cord or string to mark lines with, marking-string, tîg(-gis) (thig(-gis)) _odebs-pa ('debs pa) to use such a string, to draw lines. — 2. any instrument used in drawing lines; skor-tîg (skor thig) a pair of compasses, yya-tîg (g.ya' thig) slate-pencil, lead-pencil; also a line drawn with a lead-pencil; *tîg-ta (thig ta) taṅ-ċe* (*tang ce*) W. c. genit. to examine, try, test. — 3. a line, tîg-_odebs-pa (thig 'debs pa), rgyag-pa (rgyag pa), rgyab-pa (rgyab pa), to draw lines; guṅ-tîg (gung thig) the meridian line Cs.; nag-tîg (nag thig) or snag-tîg (snag thig) a black line, tsal-tîg (tshal thig) a red line; tsaṅs-tîg (tshangs thig) diameter; equator Cs. — 4. symb. numeral for zero. — 5. v. tîg (thig). Comp. tîg-skód (thig skod) string to mark lines with. — *tîg-nyá* (*thig nya*) W. over against. — tîg-nág (thig nag) Stg., Sch.: that part of hell, where the damned are sawn to pieces, lines being drawn upon them. — tîg-tsám (thig tsam) a little. — tîg-tsád (thig tsad) Cs. proportion, symmetry, Ld.-Glr. f. 27, 6, tîg-tsad (thig tsad) byéd-pa (byed pa) to proportion; *tîg-tsád (thig tsad) zúm-ċe* (*zum ce*) W., to determine the relation or proportion of things. — tîg-śíṅ (thig shing) a ruler, to rule lines with.

1. a spot like that of a leopard's skin, tîg-le-càn (thig le can) spotted, speckled; tîg-ma* (thig ma) W. id., of variegated woolen fabrics; čos (chos) tîg-le (thig le) nyag (nyag) ċig (cig) Mil., the centre of all religion, in which finally all the different sects must unite. — 2. zero, naught Wdk. — 3. semen virile. — 4. contemplation. The two latter significations are mystically connected with each other, as will be seen from a passage of Mil., which is also a fair specimen of the physiological and mystical reveries of the more recent Buddhism: yoṅs (yongs) lús-la (lus la) gtúm-mo (gtum mo) _obár-bas ('bar bas) bde (bde); rluṅ (rlung) ro (ro) rkyaṅ (rkyang) dhú-tir (dhu tir) čûd-pas (chud pas) bde (bde); stod (stod) byaṅ-čûb-séms-kyi (byang chub sems kyi) rgyún-_obab (rgyun 'bab) bde (bde); smad (smad) dáṅs-mai (dangs mai) tîg-le (thig le) kyáb-pas (khyab pas) bde (bde); bar (bar) dkar (dkar) dmar (dmar) tug (thug) ṕrad (phrad) brtsé-bas (brtse bas) bde (bde); lus (lus) zag-med-bdé-bas (zag med bde bas) tsím-pas (tsim pas) bde (bde); de (de) rnál-_obyor (rnal 'byor) nyáms-kyi (nyams kyi) bde (bde) drug (drug) lags (lags); he (the Yogi) feels well in general, when the warmth of meditation is kindled (cf. gtúm-mo (gtum mo)) in his body; he feels well, when the air enters through ró-ma (ro ma) and kyáṅ-ma (kyang ma) into the dhúti; he feels well in the upper part of his body by the flowing down of the bódhi; he feels well in the lower parts by the spreading of the chyle (chylous fluid, semen); he feels well in the middle, by being affected with tender compassion, when the red (the blood in the kyáṅ-ma (kyang ma)) and the white (the semen in the ró-ma (ro ma)) unite; the whole body is well, being per-
